ocr.text: The table presents a comprehensive overview of various Contig IDs alongside their corresponding annotations. The first row,
labeled CGI 10003647, is annotated as EGF-like calcium-binding protein. The second row, CGI 10005120, corresponds to NHL
repeat containing protein-binding. In the third row, CGI 10011377 is designated as glucose/ribitol dehydrogenase, while the
fourth row, CGI 10012546, has no annotation.
Contig ID  Annotation
CGI 10003647  EGF-like calcium-binding protein
CGI 10005120  NHL repeat containing protein-binding
CGI 10011377  Glucose/ribitol dehydrogenase
CGI 10012546  No annotation
CGI 10018671  Strictosidine synthase
CGI 10018819  Transient receptor potential cation channel
CGI 10018820  Similar to transient receptor potential cation channel
CGI 10022617  No annotation
CGI 10024194  Follistatin-like, protease inhibitor, Kazal-type
CGI_10028176  No annotation
CGI_10028233  Transient receptor potential cation channel
CGI 10018671, found in the fifth row, is identified as strictosidine synthase. The sixth row, CGI 10018819, is annotated as a
transient receptor potential cation channel, and the next row, CGI 10018820, is noted for being similar to a transient receptor
potential cation channel. The eighth row, CGI 10022617, again has no annotation. In the ninth row, CGI 10024194 is described
as follistatin-like, protease inhibitor, Kazal-type. The tenth row, CGI_10028176, is listed with no annotation, while the final
row, CGI_10028233, is also classified as a transient receptor potential cation channel. The content of this table provides critical
insights into the gene annotations associated with each Contig ID, highlighting both specific functions and instances of
unannotated entries, which could indicate areas for future research or analysis within the genomic context. This detailed
enumeration of both annotated and unannotated contig entries serves as a valuable resource for understanding protein functions
and their potential implications in biological processes.
